principle.name: Normalize by default
principle.description: Design schemas in normalized form unless there is evidence that denormalization is needed. Document denormalization decisions with specific rationale: query pattern, performance requirement, or consistency trade-off.
principle.rationale: Normalization reduces redundancy and update anomalies. Denormalization without evidence leads to maintenance burden.

principle.name: Index common query patterns
principle.description: Every query pattern identified in the HLD must have a corresponding index or access path. Document which queries each index supports. Avoid over-indexing — each index has write cost.
principle.rationale: Unindexed queries cause full scans and performance degradation at scale.

principle.name: Plan migrations upfront
principle.description: Define migration strategy for schema changes: additive changes, backfill requirements, downtime expectations. Document rollback approach for failed migrations.
principle.rationale: Schema evolution is inevitable; unplanned migrations cause production incidents.

principle.name: Define data lifecycle
principle.description: For each entity or data category, define retention policy, archival strategy, and deletion rules. Consider compliance (GDPR, etc.) and cost.
principle.rationale: Unbounded data growth causes cost and compliance issues.

principle.name: Consider data growth patterns
principle.description: Estimate or document expected data volume and growth rate. Design for scale: partitioning, sharding, or archival strategy if growth is significant.
principle.rationale: Designs that ignore growth fail when data scales.

pitfall.why_problematic: Adds complexity and consistency bugs without proven benefit.
pitfall.correct_approach: Start normalized; denormalize only when a specific query pattern or performance requirement justifies it.

pitfall.why_problematic: Queries degrade as data grows; full table scans are unacceptable at scale.
pitfall.correct_approach: Map each query pattern to an index; document the mapping.

pitfall.why_problematic: Schema changes become risky, ad-hoc, and error-prone.
pitfall.correct_approach: Define migration approach, rollback, and backfill strategy upfront.

pitfall.why_problematic: Data grows unbounded; compliance and cost issues emerge.
pitfall.correct_approach: Define retention, archival, and deletion for each data category.

pitfall.why_problematic: Cache invalidation complexity; stale data; unnecessary cost.
pitfall.correct_approach: Cache only when access pattern justifies it; document TTL and invalidation strategy.

category.name: before_writing_spec
category.item: All entities have attributes, types, and relationships defined.
Normalization decisions documented; denormalization has rationale.
Indexes defined for all identified query patterns.
Data lifecycle (retention, archival) defined per entity or category.
Storage technology choices documented with rationale.

category.name: schema_quality
category.item: Primary keys defined for all entities.
No orphaned entities without clear ownership.
